```{r MIUKMI}
#   "outputs/Language Invariance/EYP_language44_UK_fit_status.rds"
# )

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language_fit_status_uk,
  "outputs/Language Invariance/EYP_language44_UK_fit_status.csv"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Helper: pick robust fit index with fallback
# ------------------------------------------------------------
.pick <- function(fm, primary, fallback = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(fm[[primary]]) && !is.na(fm[[primary]])) {
    return(unname(fm[[primary]]))
  }
  if (!is.null(fallback) && !is.null(fm[[fallback]]) && !is.na(fm[[fallback]])) {
    return(unname(fm[[fallback]]))
  }
  NA_real_
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Extract fit indices
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language_fit_tbl_uk <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(model_ids_uk, function(id) {
  fit <- epq_language_models_uk[[id]]

  if (is.null(fit)) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Model      = id,
      converged  = NA,
      Chi_Square = NA_real_,
      df         = NA_real_,
      CFI        = NA_real_,
      TLI        = NA_real_,
      RMSEA      = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_L = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_U = NA_real_,
      SRMR       = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  conv <- tryCatch(lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "converged"), error = function(e) NA)
  fm   <- tryCatch(lavaan::fitMeasures(fit), error = function(e) NULL)

  if (is.null(fm)) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Model      = id,
      converged  = conv,
      Chi_Square = NA_real_,
      df         = NA_real_,
      CFI        = NA_real_,
      TLI        = NA_real_,
      RMSEA      = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_L = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_U = NA_real_,
      SRMR       = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  tibble::tibble(
    Model      = id,
    converged  = conv,
    Chi_Square = .pick(fm, "chisq.scaled",          "chisq"),
    df         = .pick(fm, "df.scaled",             "df"),
    CFI        = .pick(fm, "cfi.scaled",            "cfi"),
    TLI        = .pick(fm, "tli.scaled",            "tli"),
    RMSEA      = .pick(fm, "rmsea.scaled",          "rmsea"),
    RMSEA_90_L = .pick(fm, "rmsea.ci.lower.scaled", "rmsea.ci.lower"),
    RMSEA_90_U = .pick(fm, "rmsea.ci.upper.scaled", "rmsea.ci.upper"),
    SRMR       = .pick(fm, "srmr",                  "srmr_bentler")
  )
})) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = model_ids_uk)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Model)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. Sequential LRT
# ------------------------------------------------------------
safe_LRT <- function(prev, curr) {
  if (is.null(prev) || is.null(curr)) {
    return(c(chisq_diff = NA_real_, df_diff = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
  }

  tab <- tryCatch(lavaan::lavTestLRT(prev, curr), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(tab)) {
    return(c(chisq_diff = NA_real_, df_diff = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
  }

  tab <- as.data.frame(tab)
  i   <- nrow(tab)

  chisq <- if ("Chisq diff" %in% names(tab)) tab[["Chisq diff"]][i] else NA_real_
  df    <- if ("Df diff"    %in% names(tab)) tab[["Df diff"]][i]    else NA_real_
  p     <- if ("Pr(>Chisq)" %in% names(tab)) tab[["Pr(>Chisq)"]][i] else NA_real_

  c(
    chisq_diff = as.numeric(chisq),
    df_diff    = as.numeric(df),
    p          = as.numeric(p)
  )
}

epq_language_diff_tbl_uk <- tibble::tibble(
  Model      = model_ids_uk,
  chisq_diff = NA_real_,
  df_diff    = NA_real_,
  p          = NA_real_
)

for (i in 2:length(model_ids_uk)) {
  d <- safe_LRT(
    epq_language_models_uk[[model_ids_uk[i - 1]]],
    epq_language_models_uk[[model_ids_uk[i]]]
  )
  epq_language_diff_tbl_uk$chisq_diff[i] <- d[["chisq_diff"]]
  epq_language_diff_tbl_uk$df_diff[i]    <- d[["df_diff"]]
  epq_language_diff_tbl_uk$p[i]          <- d[["p"]]
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 12. Combined table with delta criteria
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language_invariance_table_uk <- epq_language_fit_tbl_uk %>%
  dplyr::left_join(epq_language_diff_tbl_uk, by = "Model") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dCFI             = CFI   - dplyr::lag(CFI),
    dTLI             = TLI   - dplyr::lag(TLI),
    dRMSEA           = RMSEA - dplyr::lag(RMSEA),
    CFI_invariance   = dCFI   >= -0.01,
    RMSEA_invariance = dRMSEA <=  0.01
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 13. Pretty table
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language_invariance_pretty_uk <- epq_language_invariance_table_uk %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Model,
    `Chi-Square`            = Chi_Square,
    df,
    CFI,
    TLI,
    RMSEA,
    `RMSEA 90% Lower CI`    = RMSEA_90_L,
    `RMSEA 90% Upper CI`    = RMSEA_90_U,
    `Chi-Square Difference` = chisq_diff,
    `df difference`         = df_diff,
    p,
    CFI_diff                = dCFI,
    TLI_diff                = dTLI,
    RMSEA_diff              = dRMSEA,
    CFI_invariance,
    RMSEA_invariance,
    converged
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 14. Formatting helpers
# ------------------------------------------------------------
fmt2 <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), "", sprintf("%.2f", x))
fmt3 <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), "", sprintf("%.3f", x))

apa_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(p), "",
    ifelse(p < .001, "p < .001",
           sub("^0", "", sprintf("%.3f", p)))
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 15. Final table formatter
# ------------------------------------------------------------
prep_invariance_tbl <- function(df, model_col = "Model") {
  m13_labels <- c(
    M1  = "M1 (Configural)",
    M2  = "M2 (Metric)",
    M3  = "M3 (Metric + Residuals)",
    M4  = "M4 (Metric + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M5  = "M5 (Scalar)",
    M6  = "M6 (Metric + Residuals + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M7  = "M7 (Scalar + Residuals)",
    M8  = "M8 (Scalar + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M9  = "M9 (Scalar + Residuals + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M10 = "M10 (Scalar + Means)",
    M11 = "M11 (Scalar + Residuals + Means)",
    M12 = "M12 (Scalar + Factor Var/Cov + Means)",
    M13 = "M13 (Scalar + Residuals + Factor Var/Cov + Means)"
  )

  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Model_disp = if ("Model_Label" %in% names(.)) .data[["Model_Label"]] else .data[[model_col]],
      Model_disp = dplyr::recode(
        as.character(Model_disp),
        !!!as.list(m13_labels),
        .default = as.character(Model_disp)
      ),
      RMSEA_CI = ifelse(
        is.na(.data[["RMSEA 90% Lower CI"]]) | is.na(.data[["RMSEA 90% Upper CI"]]),
        "",
        paste0(
          "[", fmt3(.data[["RMSEA 90% Lower CI"]]),
          ", ", fmt3(.data[["RMSEA 90% Upper CI"]]), "]"
        )
      )
    )

  df %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Model          = Model_disp,
      `χ²`           = fmt2(.data[["Chi-Square"]]),
      df             = ifelse(is.na(.data[["df"]]), "",
                              as.character(as.integer(.data[["df"]]))),
      CFI            = fmt3(.data[["CFI"]]),
      TLI            = fmt3(.data[["TLI"]]),
      RMSEA          = fmt3(.data[["RMSEA"]]),
      `RMSEA 90% CI` = RMSEA_CI,
      `Δχ²`          = fmt2(.data[["Chi-Square Difference"]]),
      `Δdf`          = ifelse(is.na(.data[["df difference"]]), "",
                              as.character(as.integer(.data[["df difference"]]))),
      p              = apa_p(.data[["p"]]),
      `ΔCFI`         = fmt3(.data[["CFI_diff"]]),
      `ΔTLI`         = fmt3(.data[["TLI_diff"]]),
      `ΔRMSEA`       = fmt3(.data[["RMSEA_diff"]]),
      CFI_inv        = ifelse(is.na(.data[["CFI_invariance"]]), "",
                              ifelse(.data[["CFI_invariance"]], "Yes", "No")),
      RMSEA_inv      = ifelse(is.na(.data[["RMSEA_invariance"]]), "",
                              ifelse(.data[["RMSEA_invariance"]], "Yes", "No")),
      Converged      = ifelse(is.na(.data[["converged"]]), "",
                              ifelse(.data[["converged"]], "Yes", "No"))
    )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 16. Create APA-ready table
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language44_invariance_apa_uk <- prep_invariance_tbl(epq_language_invariance_pretty_uk)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 17. Save all outputs as separate UK files
# ------------------------------------------------------------

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language_fit_tbl_uk,
  "outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_fit_table.csv"
)

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language_invariance_pretty_uk,
  "outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_invariance_raw.csv"
)

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language44_invariance_apa_uk,
  "outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_invariance_APA.csv"
)


```


```{r}
epq_language_invariance_apa <- read_csv("outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_invariance_APA.csv")
papaja::apa_table(
  epq_language_invariance_apa,
  caption = "Measurement invariance (Language) results for the EPQ-RS-B.",
  note = "Δ values are relative to the immediately preceding model. * p < .05, ** p < .01, *** p < .001."
)
```

## EPQ-RS ALIGNMENT BENCHMARKING ANALYSIS

```{r claude}
################################################################################
# EPQ-RS FACTOR ALIGNMENT BENCHMARKING  —  FINAL CORRECTED VERSION
#
# Based directly on:
#   Asparouhov, T. & Muthén, B. (2013). Multiple-group factor analysis
#   alignment. Mplus Web Notes No. 18.
#
# ── WHAT THE PAPER SPECIFIES ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
#  Model (paper eq. 1):   y_ipg = ν_pg  +  λ_pg × η_ig  +  ε_ipg
#    ν_pg  = item INTERCEPT  (higher ν → higher P(Y=1) for binary items)
#    λ_pg  = factor loading
#    η_ig ~ N(α_g, ψ_g)
#
#  For BINARY items with WLSMV + theta parameterization:
#    P(Y=1 | η) = Φ(λη − τ)   where τ = item THRESHOLD from lavaan
#    Rewrite as  = Φ(λη + ν)  where ν = −τ
#    Therefore:   ν  =  −τ                    
#
#  Alignment loss (paper eq. 4) is minimized w.r.t. α_g, ψ_g.
#  Weight (paper p. 8):  w_{g1,g2} = sqrt(N_{g1} × N_{g2})
#  sirt implements this via a G×I matrix where wgt[g, i] = sqrt(N_g).  
#
#  Reference group (FIXED alignment, paper p. 8–9):
#    α_1 = 0 fixed; sirt always uses ROW 1 as reference.
#    We REORDER so England is row 1.                                    
#
#  R² formula (sirt source sirt_rsquared.R):
#    R² = 1  −  Σ(x − expl)²  /  Σ(x²)
#    This is the no-intercept coefficient of determination, not standard R².
#    It is bounded by 1 from above and goes deeply negative when the
#    optimizer diverges (which happened because of un-negated thresholds).
#    With correct inputs it returns values near 1 and the 0.90 threshold
#    from Asparouhov & Muthén (2014) still applies.
#
#
# ── REFERENCE GROUP FILE NAME ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
#  "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data.xls"
#  tools::file_path_sans_ext strips the extension, leaving the label:
#  "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data"
#  The code matches this label exactly.
################################################################################


# ==============================================================================
# 0. PACKAGES
# ==============================================================================

required_pkgs <- c(
  "readxl", "dplyr", "purrr", "lavaan",
  "sirt", "ggplot2", "tibble", "tools", "stringr"
)

invisible(lapply(required_pkgs, function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}))


# ==============================================================================
# 1. CONFIGURATION  —  edit only these paths
# ==============================================================================

ARCHIVAL_DIR <- "EPQ- Invariance/Analysis-Files/UKscored/Total/Excel/"
BANGLA_LABEL <- "Bangladesh (Bangla)"
OUT_DIR      <- "outputs/alignment_output_final"
R2_THRESHOLD <- 0.90

# Exact label produced by tools::file_path_sans_ext on the England file.
# File name: "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data.xls"
ENGLAND_LABEL <- "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data"

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)


# ==============================================================================
# 2. VERIFY BANGLA INPUT
# ==============================================================================

if (!exists("epq_items"))
  stop("`epq_items` not found in environment. Load your Bangla item data first.")
epq_items <- as.data.frame(epq_items)


# ==============================================================================
# 3. ITEM MAP  (44 common items between IPQ-RS and EPQ-90)
# ==============================================================================

common_item_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~EYP,    ~archival_col, ~scale,
  "EYP1",  "N_3",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP3",  "E_5",  "Extraversion",
  "EYP4",  "L_13", "Lie",
  "EYP5",  "N_7",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP6",  "P_6",  "Psychoticism",
  "EYP7",  "E_10", "Extraversion",
  "EYP8",  "L_8",  "Lie",
  "EYP9",  "N_15", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP10", "P_22", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP11", "E_17", "Extraversion",
  "EYP12", "L_16", "Lie",
  "EYP13", "N_19", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP15", "E_14", "Extraversion",
  "EYP16", "L_20", "Lie",
  "EYP17", "N_23", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP18", "P_37", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP19", "E_40", "Extraversion",
  "EYP20", "L_24", "Lie",
  "EYP21", "N_31", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP22", "P_43", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP23", "E_45", "Extraversion",
  "EYP24", "L_39", "Lie",
  "EYP25", "N_34", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP27", "E_21", "Extraversion",
  "EYP28", "P_53", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP29", "L_48", "Lie",
  "EYP30", "N_41", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP31", "P_67", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP32", "E_52", "Extraversion",
  "EYP33", "L_51", "Lie",
  "EYP34", "N_72", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP35", "P_71", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP36", "E_82", "Extraversion",
  "EYP37", "L_59", "Lie",
  "EYP38", "N_75", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP39", "P_83", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP40", "L_63", "Lie",
  "EYP41", "E_42", "Extraversion",
  "EYP42", "N_77", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP44", "E_86", "Extraversion",
  "EYP45", "L_78", "Lie",
  "EYP46", "N_27", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP47", "L_85", "Lie",
  "EYP48", "E_70", "Extraversion"
)

epq_common_item_sets <- list(
  Psychoticism = c("EYP6","EYP10","EYP18","EYP22","EYP28","EYP31","EYP35","EYP39"),
  Extraversion = c("EYP3","EYP7","EYP11","EYP15","EYP19","EYP23","EYP27","EYP32",
                   "EYP36","EYP41","EYP44","EYP48"),
  Neuroticism  = c("EYP1","EYP5","EYP9","EYP13","EYP17","EYP21","EYP25","EYP30",
                   "EYP34","EYP38","EYP42","EYP46"),
  Lie          = c("EYP4","EYP8","EYP12","EYP16","EYP20","EYP24","EYP29","EYP33",
                   "EYP37","EYP40","EYP45","EYP47")
)

archival_to_eyp <- setNames(common_item_map$EYP,          common_item_map$archival_col)
eyp_to_archival <- setNames(common_item_map$archival_col, common_item_map$EYP)


# ==============================================================================
# 4. HELPER: PER-GROUP SINGLE-FACTOR WLSMV CFA
# ==============================================================================

# Returns a list with:
#   $loadings    — named vector of std. factor loadings (std.all from lavaan)
#   $thresholds  — named vector of RAW thresholds τ from lavaan (will be
#                  negated later to obtain intercepts ν = −τ)
#   $n, $converged, $items_used, $dropped_zv, $error

fit_group_cfa <- function(data_subset, factor_name, item_names,
                          group_label = "") {

  df <- as.data.frame(lapply(
    data_subset[, item_names, drop = FALSE],
    function(x) {
      if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x)) x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
      if (is.logical(x)) x <- as.integer(x)
      as.numeric(x)
    }
  ))

  # Drop zero-variance items (would crash WLSMV)
  zv <- sapply(df, function(x) length(unique(stats::na.omit(x))) < 2)
  dropped_zv <- names(df)[zv]
  df         <- df[, !zv, drop = FALSE]
  item_names <- names(df)

  if (length(dropped_zv) > 0)
    message(sprintf("  [%s | %s] zero-variance dropped: %s",
                    group_label, factor_name, paste(dropped_zv, collapse = ", ")))

  if (length(item_names) < 3)
    return(list(loadings = NULL, thresholds = NULL, n = nrow(df),
                converged = FALSE, items_used = item_names,
                dropped_zv = dropped_zv,
                error = "fewer than 3 usable items after zero-variance filter"))

  # WLSMV CFA with theta parameterization for binary items.
  # std.lv = TRUE fixes factor variance to 1 (identification).
  fit <- tryCatch(
    lavaan::cfa(
      model            = paste0(factor_name, " =~ ",
                                paste(item_names, collapse = " + ")),
      data             = df,
      estimator        = "WLSMV",
      ordered          = item_names,
      parameterization = "theta",
      std.lv           = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) e
  )

  if (inherits(fit, "error"))
    return(list(loadings = NULL, thresholds = NULL, n = nrow(df),
                converged = FALSE, items_used = item_names,
                dropped_zv = dropped_zv,
                error = conditionMessage(fit)))

  converged <- tryCatch(lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "converged"),
                        error = function(e) FALSE)
  if (!isTRUE(converged))
    return(list(loadings = NULL, thresholds = NULL, n = nrow(df),
                converged = FALSE, items_used = item_names,
                dropped_zv = dropped_zv,
                error = "lavaan did not converge"))

  pars <- lavaan::parameterEstimates(fit, standardized = TRUE)

  # Factor loadings: use std.all (fully standardised)
  lam_df   <- pars[pars$op == "=~", c("rhs", "std.all"), drop = FALSE]
  loadings <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(item_names)), item_names)
  cl       <- intersect(item_names, lam_df$rhs)
  loadings[cl] <- lam_df$std.all[match(cl, lam_df$rhs)]

  # Thresholds τ  (one per binary item, from op == "|")
  # IMPORTANT: these are RAW thresholds, NOT yet negated.
  # Negation to intercepts  ν = −τ  happens in run_alignment (FIX A).
  thr_df <- pars[pars$op == "|", c("lhs", "est"), drop = FALSE]
  thr_df <- thr_df[!duplicated(thr_df$lhs), ]         # keep first threshold
  thresholds <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(item_names)), item_names)
  ct         <- intersect(item_names, thr_df$lhs)
  thresholds[ct] <- thr_df$est[match(ct, thr_df$lhs)]

  list(
    loadings   = loadings,
    thresholds = thresholds,     # τ — caller negates to get ν
    n          = nrow(df),
    converged  = TRUE,
    items_used = item_names,
    dropped_zv = dropped_zv,
    error      = NA_character_
  )
}


# ==============================================================================
# 5. LOAD AND PROCESS ARCHIVAL FILES
# ==============================================================================

excel_files <- list.files(ARCHIVAL_DIR, pattern = "\\.xlsx?$",
                          full.names = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
if (length(excel_files) == 0)
  stop("No Excel files found in: ", ARCHIVAL_DIR)
message("Found ", length(excel_files), " archival files.")

process_country <- function(filepath) {
  label <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(filepath))
  message("\nProcessing: ", label)

  dat <- tryCatch(as.data.frame(readxl::read_excel(filepath)),
                  error = function(e) e)
  if (inherits(dat, "error")) {
    message("  READ ERROR: ", conditionMessage(dat))
    return(list(country = label, n = NA_integer_,
                results  = setNames(vector("list", 4), names(epq_common_item_sets)),
                read_error = conditionMessage(dat)))
  }

  needed    <- common_item_map$archival_col
  present   <- intersect(needed, names(dat))
  missing   <- setdiff(needed, names(dat))
  if (length(missing) > 0)
    message("  Missing archival columns: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))

  dat_c          <- dat[, present, drop = FALSE]
  names(dat_c)   <- archival_to_eyp[present]

  results <- lapply(names(epq_common_item_sets), function(sc) {
    items <- intersect(epq_common_item_sets[[sc]], names(dat_c))
    message(sprintf("  %s: %d items", sc, length(items)))
    fit_group_cfa(dat_c, sc, items, label)
  })
  names(results) <- names(epq_common_item_sets)

  list(country = label, n = nrow(dat_c),
       results = results, read_error = NA_character_)
}

archival_results <- lapply(excel_files, process_country)
names(archival_results) <- vapply(archival_results, `[[`, character(1), "country")


# ==============================================================================
# 6. PROCESS BANGLA DATA
# ==============================================================================

message("\nProcessing Bangla data from `epq_items`.")

required_bangla <- common_item_map$EYP
missing_bangla  <- setdiff(required_bangla, names(epq_items))
if (length(missing_bangla) > 0)
  message("Bangla data missing items: ", paste(missing_bangla, collapse = ", "))

bangla_dat <- as.data.frame(
  epq_items[, intersect(required_bangla, names(epq_items)), drop = FALSE]
)

bangla_cfas <- lapply(names(epq_common_item_sets), function(sc) {
  items <- intersect(epq_common_item_sets[[sc]], names(bangla_dat))
  message(sprintf("  Bangla %s: %d items", sc, length(items)))
  fit_group_cfa(bangla_dat, sc, items, BANGLA_LABEL)
})
names(bangla_cfas) <- names(epq_common_item_sets)

bangla_entry <- list(
  country    = BANGLA_LABEL,
  n          = nrow(bangla_dat),
  results    = bangla_cfas,
  read_error = NA_character_
)


# ==============================================================================
# 7. COMBINE ALL GROUPS  AND  PLACE ENGLAND FIRST  (FIX C)
#
#  sirt::invariance.alignment() always uses ROW 1 as the FIXED reference group
#  (α_1 = 0, paper p. 8-9).  We reorder so England is row 1.
#  The England file label (after stripping extension) is exactly:
#    "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data"
# ==============================================================================

all_groups  <- c(archival_results, list(Bangladesh = bangla_entry))
all_labels  <- vapply(all_groups, `[[`, character(1), "country")
all_ns      <- vapply(all_groups,
                      function(x) ifelse(is.na(x$n), 0L, as.integer(x$n)),
                      integer(1))

# Locate England by exact label match first, then partial as fallback
england_idx <- which(all_labels == ENGLAND_LABEL)
if (length(england_idx) == 0) {
  england_idx <- which(grepl("^England", all_labels, ignore.case = TRUE))
  if (length(england_idx) == 0)
    stop("England file not found. Check ENGLAND_LABEL or ARCHIVAL_DIR.\n",
         "Available labels:\n  ", paste(all_labels, collapse = "\n  "))
  warning("Exact England label not matched; using first partial match: ",
          all_labels[england_idx[1]])
}
england_idx <- england_idx[1]
message("\nReference group (row 1): ", all_labels[england_idx])

# Reorder: England first, then all others in original order
reorder_idx <- c(england_idx, setdiff(seq_along(all_groups), england_idx))
all_groups  <- all_groups[reorder_idx]
all_labels  <- all_labels[reorder_idx]
all_ns      <- all_ns[reorder_idx]

# Confirm England is now row 1
stopifnot(grepl("England", all_labels[1], ignore.case = TRUE))
message("Groups after reordering: ", length(all_groups))
message("Row 1 (reference): ", all_labels[1])
message("Bangladesh at row: ", which(all_labels == BANGLA_LABEL))


# ==============================================================================
# 8. RUN ALIGNMENT PER SUBSCALE
#
#  Paper formulas implemented:
#    ν = −τ           (FIX A: negate thresholds to get intercepts)
#    wgt[g,i] = sqrt(N_g)  as G×I matrix  (FIX B: correct sirt input type)
#    Row 1 = England  (FIX C: correct reference group)
#
#  R² interpretation (sirt_rsquared.R):
#    R² = 1 − Σ(x − expl)² / Σ(x²)
#    For loadings:   expl[g,i] = ψ_g × mean_aligned_loading[i]
#    For intercepts: expl[g,i] = mean_aligned_nu[i] + α_g × mean_aligned_loading[i]
#    Threshold ≥ 0.90 (Asparouhov & Muthén 2014)
# ==============================================================================

run_alignment <- function(scale_name) {
  message("\n=== Alignment: ", scale_name, " ===")

  target_items <- epq_common_item_sets[[scale_name]]
  G <- length(all_groups)
  I <- length(target_items)

  # ── Build parameter matrices (groups × items) ────────────────────────────
  lambda_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, G, I,
                       dimnames = list(all_labels, target_items))
  nu_mat     <- matrix(NA_real_, G, I,
                       dimnames = list(all_labels, target_items))
  # nu_mat will hold INTERCEPTS ν = −τ  (see FIX A below)

  converged_flag <- logical(G)

  for (g in seq_len(G)) {
    res <- all_groups[[g]]$results[[scale_name]]
    if (is.null(res) || !isTRUE(res$converged)) next
    converged_flag[g] <- TRUE

    lam <- res$loadings
    thr <- res$thresholds

    if (!is.null(lam)) {
      ci <- intersect(names(lam), target_items)
      lambda_mat[g, ci] <- lam[ci]
    }

    if (!is.null(thr)) {
      ci <- intersect(names(thr), target_items)
      # ── FIX A: ν = −τ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
      # lavaan returns τ (threshold, where P(Y=1) DECREASES as τ increases).
      # sirt alignment expects ν (intercept, where P(Y=1) INCREASES as ν increases).
      # Paper eq. (1): y_ipg = ν_pg + λ_pg × η_ig  →  for binary items ν = −τ.
      nu_mat[g, ci] <- -thr[ci]
    }
  }

  n_conv <- sum(converged_flag)
  message("  Groups converged: ", n_conv, " / ", G)

  if (n_conv < 2) {
    message("  Skipping: need at least 2 converged groups.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # ── Drop items with < 50% coverage across CONVERGED groups ───────────────
  coverage   <- colMeans(!is.na(lambda_mat[converged_flag, , drop = FALSE]))
  keep_items <- names(coverage)[coverage >= 0.50]
  drop_items <- setdiff(target_items, keep_items)

  if (length(drop_items) > 0)
    message("  Items dropped (< 50% coverage): ", paste(drop_items, collapse = ", "))
  if (length(keep_items) < 3) {
    message("  Skipping: fewer than 3 items with adequate coverage.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  lambda_mat <- lambda_mat[, keep_items, drop = FALSE]
  nu_mat     <- nu_mat[,    keep_items, drop = FALSE]

  # ── Keep groups that have at least one valid entry in BOTH matrices ───────
  usable <- rowSums(!is.na(lambda_mat)) > 0 & rowSums(!is.na(nu_mat)) > 0
  if (sum(usable) < 2) {
    message("  Skipping: fewer than 2 usable groups after item trim.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Confirm England (reference, row 1) is still present after filtering
  if (!usable[1]) {
    message("  WARNING: England (reference group) was dropped as unusable.",
            " Cannot proceed with this subscale.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  lambda_mat  <- lambda_mat[usable, , drop = FALSE]
  nu_mat      <- nu_mat[usable,     , drop = FALSE]
  used_labels <- rownames(lambda_mat)
  used_ns     <- all_ns[match(used_labels, all_labels)]

  # ── Mean-impute remaining NAs cell-by-cell ────────────────────────────────
  had_imputation <- anyNA(lambda_mat) || anyNA(nu_mat)
  if (had_imputation) {
    message("  Imputing remaining NA cells by column mean.")
    for (j in seq_len(ncol(lambda_mat))) {
      na_l <- is.na(lambda_mat[, j])
      na_n <- is.na(nu_mat[, j])
      if (any(na_l)) lambda_mat[na_l, j] <- mean(lambda_mat[, j], na.rm = TRUE)
      if (any(na_n)) nu_mat[na_n,     j] <- mean(nu_mat[,     j], na.rm = TRUE)
    }
  }

  # ── FIX B: Weight matrix ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Paper p.8: w_{g1,g2} = sqrt(N_{g1} × N_{g2}).
  # sirt implements this internally from a G×I matrix where wgt[g,i] = sqrt(N_g).
  # sirt IMMEDIATELY calls dim(wgt) and colSums(wgt) — it MUST be a matrix.
  # A vector has no dim and crashes the function silently (caught by tryCatch → NULL).
  #
  # Documentation example: wgt <- matrix(sqrt(Ng), length(Ng), ncol(nu))
  # This fills column-by-column, giving row g the value sqrt(N_g) in every column.
  wgt_mat <- matrix(
    sqrt(used_ns),
    nrow = length(used_ns),
    ncol = ncol(lambda_mat)      # fills column-by-column: correct
  )
  dimnames(wgt_mat) <- dimnames(lambda_mat)

  # Sanity check: first row must be England (reference)
  stopifnot(grepl("England", rownames(lambda_mat)[1], ignore.case = TRUE))

  # ── Call sirt alignment ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  align_res <- tryCatch(
    sirt::invariance.alignment(
      lambda    = lambda_mat,
      nu        = nu_mat,         # intercepts ν = −τ  (FIX A applied above)
      wgt       = wgt_mat,        # G×I matrix  (FIX B)
      align.pow = c(0.5, 0.5),   # default; matches A&M 2014 (sirt halves internally)
      eps       = 1e-4
    ),
    error   = function(e) { message("  sirt ERROR: ",   conditionMessage(e)); NULL },
    warning = function(w) { message("  sirt WARNING: ", conditionMessage(w)); NULL }
  )

  if (is.null(align_res)) {
    message("  Alignment failed. Check sirt error message above.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # ── Extract R² ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # From sirt source sirt_rsquared.R:
  #   R² = 1 − Σ(x − expl)² / Σ(x²)
  # For loadings:   x = lambda (input), expl = psi0[g] × mean_aligned_lambda[i]
  # For intercepts: x = nu     (input), expl = mean_aligned_nu[i] + alpha0[g] × mean_aligned_lambda[i]
  # Both consistent with paper equations (2) and (3).
  # Threshold ≥ 0.90 following Asparouhov & Muthén (2014).
  es <- align_res$es.invariance
  r2_load <- if (!is.null(es) && "R2" %in% rownames(es) &&
                  "loadings"   %in% colnames(es)) es["R2", "loadings"]   else NA_real_
  r2_int  <- if (!is.null(es) && "R2" %in% rownames(es) &&
                  "intercepts" %in% colnames(es)) es["R2", "intercepts"] else NA_real_

  message(sprintf("  R2 loadings = %.4f  |  R2 intercepts = %.4f  |  Acceptable: %s",
                  r2_load, r2_int,
                  ifelse(!is.na(r2_load) && r2_load >= R2_THRESHOLD, "YES", "NO")))

  list(
    scale            = scale_name,
    model            = align_res,
    items_used       = keep_items,
    groups_used      = used_labels,
    # alpha0: aligned factor means; England fixed at 0 (FIXED alignment)
    alpha0           = setNames(align_res$pars$alpha0, rownames(align_res$pars)),
    # psi0:  aligned factor scale factors (sqrt of factor variance)
    psi0             = setNames(align_res$pars$psi0,   rownames(align_res$pars)),
    itempars_aligned = align_res$itempars.aligned,
    es_invariance    = align_res$es.invariance,
    R2_loadings      = r2_load,
    R2_intercepts    = r2_int,
    acceptable       = !is.na(r2_load) && r2_load >= R2_THRESHOLD,
    had_imputation   = had_imputation
  )
}

alignment_results <- lapply(names(epq_common_item_sets), run_alignment)
names(alignment_results) <- names(epq_common_item_sets)


# ==============================================================================
# 9. OUTPUT TABLES
# ==============================================================================

# Helper: expand aligned values to the full group vector (NA for absent groups)
to_full_vec <- function(named_vec, full_labels) {
  out <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(full_labels)), full_labels)
  if (!is.null(named_vec)) out[names(named_vec)] <- named_vec
  out
}

# 9a. Aligned factor means
means_df <- tibble::tibble(Group = all_labels, n = all_ns)
for (sc in names(alignment_results)) {
  res          <- alignment_results[[sc]]
  means_df[[sc]] <- to_full_vec(if (!is.null(res)) res$alpha0 else NULL, all_labels)
}
write.csv(means_df, file.path(OUT_DIR, "aligned_factor_means.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# 9b. Aligned factor variances (psi0)
vars_df <- tibble::tibble(Group = all_labels)
for (sc in names(alignment_results)) {
  res         <- alignment_results[[sc]]
  vars_df[[sc]] <- to_full_vec(if (!is.null(res)) res$psi0 else NULL, all_labels)
}
write.csv(vars_df, file.path(OUT_DIR, "aligned_factor_vars.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# 9c. Alignment fit summary
fit_df <- tibble::tibble(
  Subscale       = names(alignment_results),
  R2_loadings    = sapply(alignment_results, function(r) if (is.null(r)) NA_real_ else r$R2_loadings),
  R2_intercepts  = sapply(alignment_results, function(r) if (is.null(r)) NA_real_ else r$R2_intercepts),
  Acceptable     = sapply(alignment_results, function(r) if (is.null(r)) NA       else r$acceptable),
  N_items_used   = sapply(alignment_results, function(r) if (is.null(r)) NA_integer_ else length(r$items_used)),
  N_groups_used  = sapply(alignment_results, function(r) if (is.null(r)) NA_integer_ else length(r$groups_used)),
  Had_imputation = sapply(alignment_results, function(r) if (is.null(r)) NA       else r$had_imputation)
)
write.csv(fit_df, file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_fit.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
message("\n=== Alignment fit ===")
print(fit_df)

# 9d. Aligned item parameters (non-invariance diagnostics)
noninv_df <- purrr::map_dfr(names(alignment_results), function(sc) {
  res <- alignment_results[[sc]]
  if (is.null(res) || is.null(res$itempars_aligned)) return(NULL)
  out          <- as.data.frame(res$itempars_aligned)
  out$Subscale <- sc
  out
})
write.csv(noninv_df, file.path(OUT_DIR, "noninvariant_params.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# 9e. Bangladesh percentile table
# Values are ALWAYS written with an Interpret column flagging acceptability.
# The R2 guard that produced all-NAs has been removed.
bangla_percentile <- purrr::map_dfr(names(alignment_results), function(sc) {
  res <- alignment_results[[sc]]

  # Alignment failed entirely for this subscale
  if (is.null(res)) {
    message("  [", sc, "] alignment NULL — all values NA.")
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Subscale = sc, R2_loadings = NA_real_, R2_intercepts = NA_real_,
      R2_acceptable = NA, Bangla_in_model = FALSE,
      Bangla_mean = NA_real_, Mean_36groups = NA_real_, SD_36groups = NA_real_,
      Percentile_rank = NA_real_, Z_score = NA_real_,
      Interpret = "DO NOT INTERPRET: alignment skipped"
    ))
  }

  in_model <- BANGLA_LABEL %in% names(res$alpha0)

  if (!in_model) {
    message("  [", sc, "] Bangladesh absent from aligned groups.",
            " Check convergence_log for Bangla ", sc, ".")
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Subscale = sc, R2_loadings = res$R2_loadings, R2_intercepts = res$R2_intercepts,
      R2_acceptable = res$acceptable, Bangla_in_model = FALSE,
      Bangla_mean = NA_real_,
      Mean_36groups = mean(unname(res$alpha0), na.rm = TRUE),
      SD_36groups   = sd(unname(res$alpha0),   na.rm = TRUE),
      Percentile_rank = NA_real_, Z_score = NA_real_,
      Interpret = "DO NOT INTERPRET: Bangladesh absent from alignment"
    ))
  }

  all_means <- unname(res$alpha0)
  bgd_mean  <- res$alpha0[[BANGLA_LABEL]]
  mu        <- mean(all_means, na.rm = TRUE)
  s         <- sd(all_means,   na.rm = TRUE)
  pct       <- round(mean(all_means <= bgd_mean, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1)
  z         <- if (!is.na(s) && s > 0) round((bgd_mean - mu) / s, 2) else NA_real_

  interp <- if (isTRUE(res$acceptable)) {
    "OK to interpret (R2 >= 0.90)"
  } else {
    sprintf("CAUTION: R2_loadings = %.3f < %.2f; interpret with caution",
            res$R2_loadings, R2_THRESHOLD)
  }

  tibble::tibble(
    Subscale = sc, R2_loadings = res$R2_loadings, R2_intercepts = res$R2_intercepts,
    R2_acceptable = res$acceptable, Bangla_in_model = TRUE,
    Bangla_mean = bgd_mean, Mean_36groups = mu, SD_36groups = s,
    Percentile_rank = pct, Z_score = z, Interpret = interp
  )
})
write.csv(bangla_percentile, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bangla_percentile.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
message("\n=== Bangladesh percentile ===")
print(bangla_percentile[, c("Subscale","R2_loadings","R2_acceptable",
                             "Bangla_mean","Percentile_rank","Z_score","Interpret")])


# ==============================================================================
# 10. CONVERGENCE LOG
# ==============================================================================

convergence_log <- purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(all_groups), function(g) {
  grp <- all_groups[[g]]
  purrr::map_dfr(names(epq_common_item_sets), function(sc) {
    res <- grp$results[[sc]]
    if (is.null(res)) {
      tibble::tibble(Group = grp$country, Subscale = sc, n = grp$n,
                     Converged = FALSE, Items_used = NA_character_,
                     Dropped_zv = NA_character_, Error = "no result object")
    } else {
      tibble::tibble(
        Group      = grp$country,  Subscale = sc,       n = res$n,
        Converged  = isTRUE(res$converged),
        Items_used = paste(res$items_used, collapse = ", "),
        Dropped_zv = paste(res$dropped_zv, collapse = ", "),
        Error      = ifelse(is.na(res$error), "", res$error)
      )
    }
  })
})
write.csv(convergence_log, file.path(OUT_DIR, "convergence_log.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

n_fail <- sum(!convergence_log$Converged)
message(sprintf("\nConvergence: %d failures out of %d group-subscale combinations.",
                n_fail, nrow(convergence_log)))


# ==============================================================================
# 11. BENCHMARK PLOT
#
# All 4 subscales shown. Panels where R2 < 0.90 get an orange strip label.
# Reference line at 0 = England.
# Bangladesh highlighted in red.
# ==============================================================================

plot_data <- purrr::map_dfr(names(alignment_results), function(sc) {
  res <- alignment_results[[sc]]
  if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)
  ok    <- isTRUE(res$acceptable)
  label <- sprintf("%s  [R\u00b2=%.3f%s]", sc, res$R2_loadings,
                   if (ok) " \u2713" else " \u26a0 CAUTION")
  tibble::tibble(
    panel     = label,
    panel_ok  = ok,
    group     = names(res$alpha0),
    mean      = unname(res$alpha0),
    is_bangla = names(res$alpha0) == BANGLA_LABEL
  )
})

if (nrow(plot_data) > 0) {
  ok_panels  <- unique(plot_data$panel[plot_data$panel_ok])
  bad_panels <- unique(plot_data$panel[!plot_data$panel_ok])
  plot_data$panel <- factor(plot_data$panel, levels = c(ok_panels, bad_panels))

  strip_fills <- setNames(
    ifelse(levels(plot_data$panel) %in% ok_panels, "#FFFFFF", "#FFE0B2"),
    levels(plot_data$panel)
  )

  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(
    plot_data,
    ggplot2::aes(x = mean, y = reorder(group, mean),
                 colour = is_bangla, size = is_bangla)
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
    ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(
      values = c("FALSE" = "grey60", "TRUE" = "#C0392B"),
      labels = c("Other group", BANGLA_LABEL), name = NULL
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_size_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 1.8, "TRUE" = 4),
                               guide = "none") +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ panel, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
                        colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.4) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title    = "Aligned Factor Means: 35 Archival Countries + Bangladesh (EPQ-RS)",
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Reference group = England (UK-scored, \u03b1 = 0).  ",
        "Red point = Bangladesh.  \u26a0 panels: R\u00b2 < ", R2_THRESHOLD,
        ", interpret with caution."
      ),
      x       = "Aligned factor mean (\u03b10)",
      y       = NULL,
      caption = paste0(
        "Alignment method: Asparouhov & Muth\u00e9n (2013, Web Note 18) ",
        "via sirt (Robitzsch).  ",
        "44 common items (IPQ-RS \u2229 EPQ-90).  ",
        "Intercepts computed as \u03bd = \u2212\u03c4 from WLSMV theta parameterization."
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.position    = "top",
      strip.background   = ggplot2::element_rect(
        fill  = strip_fills[levels(plot_data$panel)],
        color = "grey80"
      ),
      strip.text         = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
      panel.grid.major.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = ggplot2::element_blank()
    )

  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = file.path(OUT_DIR, "benchmark_plot.pdf"),
    plot     = p,
    width    = 12,
    height   = max(10, 0.22 * dplyr::n_distinct(plot_data$group)),
    device   = cairo_pdf
  )
  message("Benchmark plot saved.")
}


# ==============================================================================
# 12. SAVE RDS WORKSPACE
# ==============================================================================

saveRDS(
  list(
    common_item_map      = common_item_map,
    epq_common_item_sets = epq_common_item_sets,
    archival_results     = archival_results,
    bangla_entry         = bangla_entry,
    all_groups           = all_groups,
    all_labels           = all_labels,
    all_ns               = all_ns,
    alignment_results    = alignment_results,
    means_df             = means_df,
    vars_df              = vars_df,
    fit_df               = fit_df,
    bangla_percentile    = bangla_percentile,
    convergence_log      = convergence_log
  ),
  file = file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_workspace.rds")
)

message("\n==============================")
message("DONE. Output: ", normalizePath(OUT_DIR, winslash = "/"))
message("==============================")
message("\nKey file: ", file.path(OUT_DIR, "bangla_percentile.csv"))
message("Key file: ", file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_fit.csv"))
message("Key file: ", file.path(OUT_DIR, "benchmark_plot.pdf"))
```

```{r gpt1}
################################################################################
# EPQ-RS CROSS-CULTURAL ALIGNMENT BENCHMARKING
# R-only approximation to the logic used in:
# - McLarnon & Romero (2020)
# - Asparouhov & Muthén, Web Note 18
#
# What this script does:
#   1) Reads all archival UK-scored EPQ country files + Bangla epq_items
#   2) Keeps England as the exact reference file
#   3) Uses the common-item map you already worked with
#   4) Estimates group-specific configural 1-factor CFAs for each scale
#      using categorical/probit modeling in lavaan
#   5) Feeds the configural loadings + thresholds into sirt alignment
#   6) Tries a FREE-like alignment first, then FIXED-like if needed
#   7) Re-expresses all aligned means/SDs relative to England
#
# Important:
#   - This is the closest R-only approximation.
#   - It is NOT the exact Mplus implementation from the paper.
################################################################################

# ---- 0. PACKAGES -------------------------------------------------------------

required_pkgs <- c(
  "readxl", "dplyr", "tidyr", "purrr", "stringr", "tibble",
  "readr", "ggplot2", "lavaan", "sirt"
)

invisible(lapply(required_pkgs, function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
  }
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}))

# ---- 1. CONFIGURATION --------------------------------------------------------

ARCHIVAL_DIR   <- "EPQ- Invariance/Analysis-Files/UKscored/Total/Excel/"
OUT_DIR        <- "outputs/alignment_output_r_approx"
BANGLA_LABEL   <- "Bangladesh (Bangla)"
ENGLAND_FILE   <- "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data.xls"

# These tolerances are heuristic and only used to flag larger residual non-invariance.
# You can tighten or loosen them later.
LAMBDA_TOL <- 0.20
NU_TOL     <- 0.40

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ---- 2. VERIFY BANGLA INPUT --------------------------------------------------

if (!exists("epq_items")) {
  stop("Object `epq_items` was not found in the R environment.")
}
epq_items <- as.data.frame(epq_items)

# ---- 3. ITEM MAP -------------------------------------------------------------

common_item_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~EYP,    ~archival_col, ~scale,
  "EYP1",  "N_3",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP3",  "E_5",  "Extraversion",
  "EYP4",  "L_13", "Lie",
  "EYP5",  "N_7",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP6",  "P_6",  "Psychoticism",
  "EYP7",  "E_10", "Extraversion",
  "EYP8",  "L_8",  "Lie",
  "EYP9",  "N_15", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP10", "P_22", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP11", "E_17", "Extraversion",
  "EYP12", "L_16", "Lie",
  "EYP13", "N_19", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP15", "E_14", "Extraversion",
  "EYP16", "L_20", "Lie",
  "EYP17", "N_23", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP18", "P_37", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP19", "E_40", "Extraversion",
  "EYP20", "L_24", "Lie",
  "EYP21", "N_31", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP22", "P_43", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP23", "E_45", "Extraversion",
  "EYP24", "L_39", "Lie",
  "EYP25", "N_34", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP27", "E_21", "Extraversion",
  "EYP28", "P_53", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP29", "L_48", "Lie",
  "EYP30", "N_41", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP31", "P_67", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP32", "E_52", "Extraversion",
  "EYP33", "L_51", "Lie",
  "EYP34", "N_72", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP35", "P_71", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP36", "E_82", "Extraversion",
  "EYP37", "L_59", "Lie",
  "EYP38", "N_75", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP39", "P_83", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP40", "L_63", "Lie",
  "EYP41", "E_42", "Extraversion",
  "EYP42", "N_77", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP44", "E_86", "Extraversion",
  "EYP45", "L_78", "Lie",
  "EYP46", "N_27", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP47", "L_85", "Lie",
  "EYP48", "E_70", "Extraversion"
)

epq_common_item_sets <- list(
  Psychoticism = c("EYP6","EYP10","EYP18","EYP22","EYP28","EYP31","EYP35","EYP39"),
  Extraversion = c("EYP3","EYP7","EYP11","EYP15","EYP19","EYP23","EYP27","EYP32",
                   "EYP36","EYP41","EYP44","EYP48"),
  Neuroticism  = c("EYP1","EYP5","EYP9","EYP13","EYP17","EYP21","EYP25","EYP30",
                   "EYP34","EYP38","EYP42","EYP46"),
  Lie          = c("EYP4","EYP8","EYP12","EYP16","EYP20","EYP24","EYP29","EYP33",
                   "EYP37","EYP40","EYP45","EYP47")
)

archival_to_eyp <- setNames(common_item_map$EYP, common_item_map$archival_col)

# ---- 4. HELPERS --------------------------------------------------------------

infer_country_from_filename <- function(path) {
  x <- basename(path)
  x <- stringr::str_remove(x, "\\.[^.]+$")
  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, "_", " ")
  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, "-", " ")
  x <- stringr::str_replace(
    x,
    regex("total sample uk scored epq item data", ignore_case = TRUE),
    ""
  )
  stringr::str_squish(x)
}

normalize_binary <- function(x) {
  if (is.factor(x)) x <- as.character(x)
  if (is.logical(x)) x <- as.integer(x)

  if (is.character(x)) {
    x_clean <- stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_trim(x))
    x <- dplyr::case_when(
      x_clean %in% c("yes", "y", "1", "true", "t") ~ 1,
      x_clean %in% c("no",  "n", "0", "false", "f") ~ 0,
      x_clean %in% c("", "na", "n/a", ".", "missing") ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_clean))
    )
  } else {
    x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
  }

  vals <- sort(unique(stats::na.omit(x)))

  # Common archival coding from Eysenck & Barrett (2013)-style files:
  # 1 = No, 3 = Yes, 2 = missing
  if (all(vals %in% c(1, 2, 3))) {
    x <- dplyr::case_when(
      x == 1 ~ 0,
      x == 3 ~ 1,
      x == 2 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE   ~ x
    )
    return(x)
  }

  if (identical(vals, c(1, 2))) {
    x <- x - 1
    return(x)
  }

  if (identical(vals, c(1, 3))) {
    x <- ifelse(is.na(x), NA_real_, ifelse(x == 3, 1, 0))
    return(x)
  }

  bad <- setdiff(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(x))), c(0, 1))
  if (length(bad) > 0) {
    warning("Non-binary values detected: ", paste(bad, collapse = ", "))
  }

  x
}

coerce_binary_df <- function(df) {
  as.data.frame(lapply(df, normalize_binary))
}

read_archival_country <- function(path) {
  raw <- readxl::read_excel(path)
  raw <- as.data.frame(raw)
  names(raw) <- stringr::str_squish(names(raw))

  needed <- common_item_map$archival_col
  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(raw))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Missing archival columns in ", basename(path), ": ",
         paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  dat <- raw[, needed, drop = FALSE]
  names(dat) <- archival_to_eyp[names(dat)]
  dat <- coerce_binary_df(dat)

  list(
    country = infer_country_from_filename(path),
    source_file = path,
    data = dat,
    n_raw = nrow(dat)
  )
}

read_bangla_country <- function(epq_items_df) {
  needed <- common_item_map$EYP
  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(epq_items_df))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Bangla epq_items is missing: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  dat <- epq_items_df[, needed, drop = FALSE]
  dat <- coerce_binary_df(dat)

  list(
    country = BANGLA_LABEL,
    source_file = NA_character_,
    data = dat,
    n_raw = nrow(dat)
  )
}

fit_configural_group_cfa <- function(df, items, scale_name, country_name) {
  dat <- df[, items, drop = FALSE]
  dat <- dat[stats::complete.cases(dat), , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(dat) == 0) {
    stop("No complete cases for ", scale_name, " in ", country_name)
  }

  bad_items <- names(which(vapply(dat, function(x) length(unique(stats::na.omit(x))) < 2, logical(1))))
  if (length(bad_items) > 0) {
    stop(
      "Zero-variance item(s) in ", country_name, " for ", scale_name, ": ",
      paste(bad_items, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }

  model_syntax <- paste0("F =~ ", paste(items, collapse = " + "))

  fit <- lavaan::cfa(
    model = model_syntax,
    data = dat,
    ordered = items,
    estimator = "WLSMV",
    parameterization = "theta",
    std.lv = TRUE
  )

  pe <- lavaan::parameterEstimates(fit, standardized = FALSE)

  # Loadings
  loading_df <- pe |>
    dplyr::filter(op == "=~", lhs == "F") |>
    dplyr::select(rhs, est)

  lambda <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(items)), items)
  lambda[loading_df$rhs] <- loading_df$est

  # Thresholds: binary items should have one threshold
  thr_df <- pe |>
    dplyr::filter(op == "|") |>
    dplyr::select(lhs, est) |>
    dplyr::distinct(lhs, .keep_all = TRUE)

  tau <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(items)), items)
  common_items <- intersect(items, thr_df$lhs)
  tau[common_items] <- thr_df$est[match(common_items, thr_df$lhs)]

  # Alignment works on intercept-like parameters.
  # For binary probit CFA, use nu = -tau as the practical conversion.
  nu <- -tau

  list(
    fit = fit,
    n_complete = nrow(dat),
    lambda = lambda,
    nu = nu
  )
}

run_alignment_one_scale <- function(scale_name, all_groups, group_key) {
  message("\n==============================")
  message("Running scale: ", scale_name)
  message("==============================")

  items <- epq_common_item_sets[[scale_name]]

  config_est <- purrr::map(
    all_groups,
    ~ fit_configural_group_cfa(
      df = .x$data,
      items = items,
      scale_name = scale_name,
      country_name = .x$country
    )
  )

  lambda <- do.call(rbind, purrr::map(config_est, "lambda"))
  nu     <- do.call(rbind, purrr::map(config_est, "nu"))
  n_vec  <- purrr::map_int(config_est, "n_complete")

  rownames(lambda) <- group_key$country
  rownames(nu)     <- group_key$country
  colnames(lambda) <- items
  colnames(nu)     <- items

  # Web Note 18 uses weights based on sqrt(Ng1 * Ng2) in the loss function.
  # In sirt, weights are supplied as a G x I matrix, commonly with sqrt(Ng).
  wgt <- matrix(sqrt(n_vec), nrow = length(n_vec), ncol = length(items))
  colnames(wgt) <- items
  rownames(wgt) <- group_key$country

  # FREE-like first
  free_res <- tryCatch(
    sirt::invariance.alignment(
      lambda = lambda,
      nu = nu,
      wgt = wgt,
      align.scale = c(1, 1),
      align.pow = c(.5, .5),
      center = FALSE,
      fixed = FALSE,
      meth = 4
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  chosen <- free_res
  chosen_method <- "FREE-like (sirt meth=4)"
  fallback_used <- FALSE

  if (is.null(chosen)) {
    fallback_used <- TRUE
  } else {
    bad_pars <- any(!is.finite(chosen$pars$alpha0)) ||
      any(!is.finite(chosen$pars$psi0)) ||
      any(chosen$pars$psi0 <= 0)

    if (bad_pars) fallback_used <- TRUE
  }

  if (fallback_used) {
    chosen <- sirt::invariance.alignment(
      lambda = lambda,
      nu = nu,
      wgt = wgt,
      align.scale = c(1, 1),
      align.pow = c(.5, .5),
      center = FALSE,
      fixed = TRUE,
      meth = 3
    )
    chosen_method <- "FIXED-like (sirt meth=3)"
  }

  pars <- as.data.frame(chosen$pars)
  pars$country <- group_key$country
  pars$scale <- scale_name
  pars$method <- chosen_method
  pars$n_complete <- n_vec

  # Re-express on England-referenced metric
  eng_idx <- which(group_key$is_reference)
  alpha_eng <- pars$alpha0[eng_idx]
  psi_eng   <- pars$psi0[eng_idx]

  pars$mean_eng_ref     <- (pars$alpha0 - alpha_eng) / psi_eng
  pars$sd_eng_ref       <- pars$psi0 / psi_eng
  pars$variance_eng_ref <- pars$sd_eng_ref^2

  # Effect sizes of approximate invariance
  es <- as.data.frame(chosen$es.invariance)
  es$scale <- scale_name
  es$method <- chosen_method

  # Residual non-invariance tables
  lambda_resid <- as.data.frame(chosen$lambda.resid)
  lambda_resid$country <- rownames(lambda_resid)

  nu_resid <- as.data.frame(chosen$nu.resid)
  nu_resid$country <- rownames(nu_resid)

  lambda_noninv <- lambda_resid |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-country, names_to = "item", values_to = "resid") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      parameter = "loading",
      abs_resid = abs(resid),
      flagged = abs_resid > LAMBDA_TOL,
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method
    )

  nu_noninv <- nu_resid |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-country, names_to = "item", values_to = "resid") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      parameter = "threshold/intercept",
      abs_resid = abs(resid),
      flagged = abs_resid > NU_TOL,
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method
    )

  noninv <- dplyr::bind_rows(lambda_noninv, nu_noninv)

  config_lambda <- as.data.frame(lambda)
  config_lambda$country <- rownames(config_lambda)
  config_lambda$scale <- scale_name

  config_nu <- as.data.frame(nu)
  config_nu$country <- rownames(config_nu)
  config_nu$scale <- scale_name

  list(
    scale = scale_name,
    items = items,
    method = chosen_method,
    config_est = config_est,
    config_lambda = config_lambda,
    config_nu = config_nu,
    alignment = chosen,
    pars = pars,
    es = es,
    noninv = noninv
  )
}

# ---- 5. READ GROUPS ----------------------------------------------------------

archival_files <- list.files(
  ARCHIVAL_DIR,
  pattern = "\\.(xls|xlsx)$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

if (length(archival_files) == 0) {
  stop("No archival Excel files found in: ", ARCHIVAL_DIR)
}

archival_groups <- purrr::map(archival_files, read_archival_country)
bangla_group    <- read_bangla_country(epq_items)

all_groups <- c(archival_groups, list(bangla_group))

source_files <- purrr::map_chr(
  all_groups,
  ~ if (is.null(.x$source_file) || all(is.na(.x$source_file))) "" else basename(.x$source_file)
)

eng_idx <- which(source_files == ENGLAND_FILE)

if (length(eng_idx) == 0) {
  stop("England reference file was not found. Expected file name: ", ENGLAND_FILE)
}

reorder_idx <- c(eng_idx[1], setdiff(seq_along(all_groups), eng_idx[1]))
all_groups  <- all_groups[reorder_idx]

group_key <- tibble::tibble(
  code = seq_along(all_groups),
  country = purrr::map_chr(all_groups, "country"),
  n_raw = purrr::map_int(all_groups, "n_raw"),
  source_file = purrr::map_chr(
    all_groups,
    ~ if (is.null(.x$source_file) || all(is.na(.x$source_file))) "" else basename(.x$source_file)
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    is_bangla = country == BANGLA_LABEL,
    is_reference = source_file == ENGLAND_FILE
  )

readr::write_csv(group_key, file.path(OUT_DIR, "country_key.csv"))

message(
  "England reference group: ",
  group_key$country[group_key$is_reference][1],
  " [", group_key$source_file[group_key$is_reference][1], "]"
)

# ---- 6. RUN ALIGNMENT --------------------------------------------------------

alignment_results <- purrr::map(
  names(epq_common_item_sets),
  ~ run_alignment_one_scale(.x, all_groups = all_groups, group_key = group_key)
)
names(alignment_results) <- names(epq_common_item_sets)

# ---- 7. EXPORT RESULTS -------------------------------------------------------

aligned_means <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "pars"))
alignment_es  <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "es"))
noninv_tbl    <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "noninv"))
config_lambda <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "config_lambda"))
config_nu     <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "config_nu"))

readr::write_csv(aligned_means, file.path(OUT_DIR, "aligned_factor_means_variances.csv"))
readr::write_csv(alignment_es,  file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_effect_sizes.csv"))
readr::write_csv(noninv_tbl,    file.path(OUT_DIR, "noninvariant_params.csv"))
readr::write_csv(config_lambda, file.path(OUT_DIR, "configural_loadings.csv"))
readr::write_csv(config_nu,     file.path(OUT_DIR, "configural_nu.csv"))

# ---- 8. BANGLA PERCENTILE ----------------------------------------------------

bangla_percentile <- aligned_means |>
  dplyr::group_by(scale) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    z_against_groups = as.numeric(scale(mean_eng_ref)),
    percentile = dplyr::percent_rank(mean_eng_ref) * 100
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(country == BANGLA_LABEL)

readr::write_csv(bangla_percentile, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bangla_percentile.csv"))

# ---- 9. SUMMARY OF FLAGGED NONINVARIANCE ------------------------------------

noninv_summary <- noninv_tbl |>
  dplyr::group_by(scale, parameter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    flagged_n = sum(flagged, na.rm = TRUE),
    total = dplyr::n(),
    pct_flagged = 100 * flagged_n / total,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

readr::write_csv(noninv_summary, file.path(OUT_DIR, "noninvariance_summary.csv"))

# ---- 10. PLOT ----------------------------------------------------------------

plot_df <- aligned_means |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    is_bangla = country == BANGLA_LABEL,
    scale = factor(scale, levels = c("Psychoticism", "Extraversion", "Neuroticism", "Lie"))
  )

p <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  plot_df,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = mean_eng_ref,
    y = reorder(country, mean_eng_ref),
    colour = is_bangla,
    size = is_bangla
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ scale, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "grey60", "TRUE" = "#C0392B"),
    labels = c("Other group", BANGLA_LABEL),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_size_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 1.8, "TRUE" = 4), guide = "none") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Aligned factor means relative to England",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "R-only approximation to McLarnon/Web Note 18 logic; England reference file = ",
      ENGLAND_FILE
    ),
    x = "Aligned latent mean (England = 0)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold")
  )

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(OUT_DIR, "benchmark_plot.pdf"),
  plot = p,
  width = 12,
  height = max(10, 0.22 * dplyr::n_distinct(plot_df$country))
)

# ---- 11. SAVE WORKSPACE ------------------------------------------------------

saveRDS(
  list(
    group_key = group_key,
    alignment_results = alignment_results,
    aligned_means = aligned_means,
    alignment_es = alignment_es,
    noninv_tbl = noninv_tbl,
    noninv_summary = noninv_summary,
    bangla_percentile = bangla_percentile
  ),
  file = file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_workspace.rds")
)

message("\nDone. Output folder: ", normalizePath(OUT_DIR, winslash = "/"))
```

```{r gpt2}
################################################################################
# EPQ-RS CROSS-CULTURAL ALIGNMENT BENCHMARKING
# R-only approximation to the logic used in:
# - McLarnon & Romero (2020)
# - Asparouhov & Muthén, Web Note 18
#
# What this script does:
#   1) Reads all archival UK-scored EPQ country files + Bangla epq_items
#   2) Keeps England as the exact reference file
#   3) Uses the common-item map
#   4) Estimates group-specific configural 1-factor CFAs for each scale
#      using categorical/probit-style ordered-item modeling in lavaan
#   5) Feeds configural loadings + thresholds into sirt alignment
#   6) Tries a FREE-like alignment first, then FIXED-like if needed
#   7) Re-expresses aligned means/SDs relative to England
#   8) Exports alignment effect sizes and R2 summaries
#
# Important:
#   - This is the closest R-only approximation.
#   - It is NOT the exact Mplus implementation from the paper.
################################################################################

# ---- 0. PACKAGES -------------------------------------------------------------

required_pkgs <- c(
  "readxl", "dplyr", "tidyr", "purrr", "stringr", "tibble",
  "readr", "ggplot2", "lavaan", "sirt"
)

invisible(lapply(required_pkgs, function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
  }
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}))

# ---- 1. CONFIGURATION --------------------------------------------------------

ARCHIVAL_DIR   <- "EPQ- Invariance/Analysis-Files/UKscored/Total/Excel/"
OUT_DIR        <- "alignment_output_r_approx"
BANGLA_LABEL   <- "Bangladesh (Bangla)"
ENGLAND_FILE   <- "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data.xls"

# Heuristic flags for larger residual non-invariance
LAMBDA_TOL <- 0.20
NU_TOL     <- 0.40

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ---- 2. VERIFY BANGLA INPUT --------------------------------------------------

if (!exists("epq_items")) {
  stop("Object `epq_items` was not found in the R environment.")
}
epq_items <- as.data.frame(epq_items)

# ---- 3. ITEM MAP -------------------------------------------------------------

common_item_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~EYP,    ~archival_col, ~scale,
  "EYP1",  "N_3",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP3",  "E_5",  "Extraversion",
  "EYP4",  "L_13", "Lie",
  "EYP5",  "N_7",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP6",  "P_6",  "Psychoticism",
  "EYP7",  "E_10", "Extraversion",
  "EYP8",  "L_8",  "Lie",
  "EYP9",  "N_15", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP10", "P_22", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP11", "E_17", "Extraversion",
  "EYP12", "L_16", "Lie",
  "EYP13", "N_19", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP15", "E_14", "Extraversion",
  "EYP16", "L_20", "Lie",
  "EYP17", "N_23", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP18", "P_37", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP19", "E_40", "Extraversion",
  "EYP20", "L_24", "Lie",
  "EYP21", "N_31", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP22", "P_43", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP23", "E_45", "Extraversion",
  "EYP24", "L_39", "Lie",
  "EYP25", "N_34", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP27", "E_21", "Extraversion",
  "EYP28", "P_53", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP29", "L_48", "Lie",
  "EYP30", "N_41", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP31", "P_67", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP32", "E_52", "Extraversion",
  "EYP33", "L_51", "Lie",
  "EYP34", "N_72", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP35", "P_71", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP36", "E_82", "Extraversion",
  "EYP37", "L_59", "Lie",
  "EYP38", "N_75", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP39", "P_83", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP40", "L_63", "Lie",
  "EYP41", "E_42", "Extraversion",
  "EYP42", "N_77", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP44", "E_86", "Extraversion",
  "EYP45", "L_78", "Lie",
  "EYP46", "N_27", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP47", "L_85", "Lie",
  "EYP48", "E_70", "Extraversion"
)

epq_common_item_sets <- list(
  Psychoticism = c("EYP6","EYP10","EYP18","EYP22","EYP28","EYP31","EYP35","EYP39"),
  Extraversion = c("EYP3","EYP7","EYP11","EYP15","EYP19","EYP23","EYP27","EYP32",
                   "EYP36","EYP41","EYP44","EYP48"),
  Neuroticism  = c("EYP1","EYP5","EYP9","EYP13","EYP17","EYP21","EYP25","EYP30",
                   "EYP34","EYP38","EYP42","EYP46"),
  Lie          = c("EYP4","EYP8","EYP12","EYP16","EYP20","EYP24","EYP29","EYP33",
                   "EYP37","EYP40","EYP45","EYP47")
)

archival_to_eyp <- setNames(common_item_map$EYP, common_item_map$archival_col)

# ---- 4. HELPERS --------------------------------------------------------------

infer_country_from_filename <- function(path) {
  x <- basename(path)
  x <- stringr::str_remove(x, "\\.[^.]+$")
  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, "_", " ")
  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, "-", " ")
  x <- stringr::str_replace(
    x,
    regex("total sample uk scored epq item data", ignore_case = TRUE),
    ""
  )
  stringr::str_squish(x)
}

normalize_binary <- function(x) {
  if (is.factor(x)) x <- as.character(x)
  if (is.logical(x)) x <- as.integer(x)

  if (is.character(x)) {
    x_clean <- stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_trim(x))
    x <- dplyr::case_when(
      x_clean %in% c("yes", "y", "1", "true", "t") ~ 1,
      x_clean %in% c("no",  "n", "0", "false", "f") ~ 0,
      x_clean %in% c("", "na", "n/a", ".", "missing") ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_clean))
    )
  } else {
    x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
  }

  vals <- sort(unique(stats::na.omit(x)))

  # Common archival coding: 1 = No, 3 = Yes, 2 = missing
  if (all(vals %in% c(1, 2, 3))) {
    x <- dplyr::case_when(
      x == 1 ~ 0,
      x == 3 ~ 1,
      x == 2 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE   ~ x
    )
    return(x)
  }

  if (identical(vals, c(1, 2))) {
    x <- x - 1
    return(x)
  }

  if (identical(vals, c(1, 3))) {
    x <- ifelse(is.na(x), NA_real_, ifelse(x == 3, 1, 0))
    return(x)
  }

  bad <- setdiff(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(x))), c(0, 1))
  if (length(bad) > 0) {
    warning("Non-binary values detected: ", paste(bad, collapse = ", "))
  }

  x
}

coerce_binary_df <- function(df) {
  as.data.frame(lapply(df, normalize_binary))
}

read_archival_country <- function(path) {
  raw <- readxl::read_excel(path)
  raw <- as.data.frame(raw)
  names(raw) <- stringr::str_squish(names(raw))

  needed <- common_item_map$archival_col
  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(raw))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Missing archival columns in ", basename(path), ": ",
         paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  dat <- raw[, needed, drop = FALSE]
  names(dat) <- archival_to_eyp[names(dat)]
  dat <- coerce_binary_df(dat)

  list(
    country = infer_country_from_filename(path),
    source_file = path,
    data = dat,
    n_raw = nrow(dat)
  )
}

read_bangla_country <- function(epq_items_df) {
  needed <- common_item_map$EYP
  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(epq_items_df))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Bangla epq_items is missing: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  dat <- epq_items_df[, needed, drop = FALSE]
  dat <- coerce_binary_df(dat)

  list(
    country = BANGLA_LABEL,
    source_file = NA_character_,
    data = dat,
    n_raw = nrow(dat)
  )
}

fit_configural_group_cfa <- function(df, items, scale_name, country_name) {
  dat <- df[, items, drop = FALSE]
  dat <- dat[stats::complete.cases(dat), , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(dat) == 0) {
    stop("No complete cases for ", scale_name, " in ", country_name)
  }

  bad_items <- names(which(vapply(dat, function(x) length(unique(stats::na.omit(x))) < 2, logical(1))))
  if (length(bad_items) > 0) {
    stop(
      "Zero-variance item(s) in ", country_name, " for ", scale_name, ": ",
      paste(bad_items, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }

  model_syntax <- paste0("F =~ ", paste(items, collapse = " + "))

  fit <- lavaan::cfa(
    model = model_syntax,
    data = dat,
    ordered = items,
    estimator = "WLSMV",
    parameterization = "theta",
    std.lv = TRUE
  )

  if (!lavaan::inspect(fit, "converged")) {
    stop("lavaan CFA did not converge for ", scale_name, " in ", country_name)
  }

  pe <- lavaan::parameterEstimates(fit, standardized = FALSE)

  loading_df <- pe |>
    dplyr::filter(op == "=~", lhs == "F") |>
    dplyr::select(rhs, est)

  lambda <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(items)), items)
  lambda[loading_df$rhs] <- loading_df$est

  thr_df <- pe |>
    dplyr::filter(op == "|") |>
    dplyr::select(lhs, est) |>
    dplyr::distinct(lhs, .keep_all = TRUE)

  tau <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(items)), items)
  common_items <- intersect(items, thr_df$lhs)
  tau[common_items] <- thr_df$est[match(common_items, thr_df$lhs)]

  # For binary categorical CFA, use intercept-like parameter nu = -tau
  nu <- -tau

  list(
    fit = fit,
    n_complete = nrow(dat),
    lambda = lambda,
    nu = nu
  )
}

extract_alignment_es_r2 <- function(alignment_obj, scale_name, chosen_method) {
  es_obj <- alignment_obj$es.invariance

  # Robust coercion to matrix/data.frame
  es_df <- as.data.frame(es_obj)

  # If rownames are missing, try to infer them
  if (is.null(rownames(es_df)) || all(rownames(es_df) == as.character(seq_len(nrow(es_df))))) {
    if (nrow(es_df) == 3) {
      rownames(es_df) <- c("R2", "sqrtU2", "rbar")
    }
  }

  es_df$metric <- rownames(es_df)
  rownames(es_df) <- NULL

  # Normalize likely column names
  names(es_df) <- gsub("\\.+", "_", names(es_df))
  names(es_df) <- tolower(names(es_df))

  # Try to map columns to expected parameters
  col_map <- names(es_df)
  col_map[col_map %in% c("loadings", "loading", "lambda")] <- "loading"
  col_map[col_map %in% c("intercepts", "intercept", "nu", "threshold", "thresholds")] <- "threshold/intercept"
  names(es_df) <- col_map

  # Keep only known columns plus metric
  keep_cols <- intersect(names(es_df), c("metric", "loading", "threshold/intercept"))
  es_df <- es_df[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]

  es_long <- es_df |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = -metric,
      names_to = "parameter",
      values_to = "value"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method,
      .before = 1
    )

  r2_tbl <- es_long |>
    dplyr::filter(metric == "R2") |>
    dplyr::select(scale, method, parameter, value) |>
    dplyr::rename(R2 = value)

  r2_summary <- r2_tbl |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = parameter,
      values_from = R2
    )

  # Ensure columns exist
  if (!"loading" %in% names(r2_summary)) {
    r2_summary$loading <- NA_real_
  }
  if (!"threshold/intercept" %in% names(r2_summary)) {
    r2_summary$`threshold/intercept` <- NA_real_
  }

  r2_summary <- r2_summary |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      min_R2 = pmin(loading, `threshold/intercept`, na.rm = TRUE),
      acceptable_by_R2_90 = dplyr::if_else(
        !is.na(loading) &
          !is.na(`threshold/intercept`) &
          loading >= .90 &
          `threshold/intercept` >= .90,
        TRUE, FALSE,
        missing = FALSE
      )
    )

  list(
    es_long = es_long,
    r2_tbl = r2_tbl,
    r2_summary = r2_summary
  )
}

run_alignment_one_scale <- function(scale_name, all_groups, group_key) {
  message("\n==============================")
  message("Running scale: ", scale_name)
  message("==============================")

  items <- epq_common_item_sets[[scale_name]]

  config_est <- purrr::map(
    all_groups,
    ~ fit_configural_group_cfa(
      df = .x$data,
      items = items,
      scale_name = scale_name,
      country_name = .x$country
    )
  )

  lambda <- do.call(rbind, purrr::map(config_est, "lambda"))
  nu     <- do.call(rbind, purrr::map(config_est, "nu"))
  n_vec  <- purrr::map_int(config_est, "n_complete")

  rownames(lambda) <- group_key$country
  rownames(nu)     <- group_key$country
  colnames(lambda) <- items
  colnames(nu)     <- items

  # Weighting based on group sample sizes
  wgt <- matrix(sqrt(n_vec), nrow = length(n_vec), ncol = length(items))
  colnames(wgt) <- items
  rownames(wgt) <- group_key$country

  # FREE-like first
  free_res <- tryCatch(
    sirt::invariance.alignment(
      lambda = lambda,
      nu = nu,
      wgt = wgt,
      align.scale = c(1, 1),
      align.pow = c(.5, .5),
      center = FALSE,
      fixed = FALSE,
      meth = 4
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  chosen <- free_res
  chosen_method <- "FREE-like (sirt meth=4)"
  fallback_used <- FALSE

  if (is.null(chosen)) {
    fallback_used <- TRUE
  } else {
    bad_pars <- any(!is.finite(chosen$pars$alpha0)) ||
      any(!is.finite(chosen$pars$psi0)) ||
      any(chosen$pars$psi0 <= 0)

    if (bad_pars) fallback_used <- TRUE
  }

  if (fallback_used) {
    chosen <- sirt::invariance.alignment(
      lambda = lambda,
      nu = nu,
      wgt = wgt,
      align.scale = c(1, 1),
      align.pow = c(.5, .5),
      center = FALSE,
      fixed = TRUE,
      meth = 3
    )
    chosen_method <- "FIXED-like (sirt meth=3)"
  }

  pars <- as.data.frame(chosen$pars)
  pars$country <- group_key$country
  pars$scale <- scale_name
  pars$method <- chosen_method
  pars$n_complete <- n_vec

  # Re-express on England-referenced metric
  eng_idx <- which(group_key$is_reference)
  alpha_eng <- pars$alpha0[eng_idx]
  psi_eng   <- pars$psi0[eng_idx]

  pars$mean_eng_ref     <- (pars$alpha0 - alpha_eng) / psi_eng
  pars$sd_eng_ref       <- pars$psi0 / psi_eng
  pars$variance_eng_ref <- pars$sd_eng_ref^2

  # Alignment effect sizes and R2
  es_r2_out <- extract_alignment_es_r2(chosen, scale_name, chosen_method)
  es         <- es_r2_out$es_long
  r2_tbl     <- es_r2_out$r2_tbl
  r2_summary <- es_r2_out$r2_summary

  # Residual non-invariance tables
  lambda_resid <- as.data.frame(chosen$lambda.resid)
  lambda_resid$country <- rownames(lambda_resid)

  nu_resid <- as.data.frame(chosen$nu.resid)
  nu_resid$country <- rownames(nu_resid)

  lambda_noninv <- lambda_resid |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-country, names_to = "item", values_to = "resid") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      parameter = "loading",
      abs_resid = abs(resid),
      flagged = abs_resid > LAMBDA_TOL,
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method
    )

  nu_noninv <- nu_resid |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-country, names_to = "item", values_to = "resid") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      parameter = "threshold/intercept",
      abs_resid = abs(resid),
      flagged = abs_resid > NU_TOL,
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method
    )

  noninv <- dplyr::bind_rows(lambda_noninv, nu_noninv)

  config_lambda <- as.data.frame(lambda)
  config_lambda$country <- rownames(config_lambda)
  config_lambda$scale <- scale_name

  config_nu <- as.data.frame(nu)
  config_nu$country <- rownames(config_nu)
  config_nu$scale <- scale_name

  list(
    scale = scale_name,
    items = items,
    method = chosen_method,
    config_est = config_est,
    config_lambda = config_lambda,
    config_nu = config_nu,
    alignment = chosen,
    pars = pars,
    es = es,
    r2_tbl = r2_tbl,
    r2_summary = r2_summary,
    noninv = noninv
  )
}

# ---- 5. READ GROUPS ----------------------------------------------------------

archival_files <- list.files(
  ARCHIVAL_DIR,
  pattern = "\\.(xls|xlsx)$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

if (length(archival_files) == 0) {
  stop("No archival Excel files found in: ", ARCHIVAL_DIR)
}

archival_groups <- purrr::map(archival_files, read_archival_country)
bangla_group    <- read_bangla_country(epq_items)

all_groups <- c(archival_groups, list(bangla_group))

source_files <- purrr::map_chr(
  all_groups,
  ~ if (is.null(.x$source_file) || all(is.na(.x$source_file))) "" else basename(.x$source_file)
)

eng_idx <- which(source_files == ENGLAND_FILE)

if (length(eng_idx) == 0) {
  stop("England reference file was not found. Expected file name: ", ENGLAND_FILE)
}

reorder_idx <- c(eng_idx[1], setdiff(seq_along(all_groups), eng_idx[1]))
all_groups  <- all_groups[reorder_idx]

group_key <- tibble::tibble(
  code = seq_along(all_groups),
  country = purrr::map_chr(all_groups, "country"),
  n_raw = purrr::map_int(all_groups, "n_raw"),
  source_file = purrr::map_chr(
    all_groups,
    ~ if (is.null(.x$source_file) || all(is.na(.x$source_file))) "" else basename(.x$source_file)
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    is_bangla = country == BANGLA_LABEL,
    is_reference = source_file == ENGLAND_FILE
  )

readr::write_csv(group_key, file.path(OUT_DIR, "country_key.csv"))

message(
  "England reference group: ",
  group_key$country[group_key$is_reference][1],
  " [", group_key$source_file[group_key$is_reference][1], "]"
)

# ---- 6. RUN ALIGNMENT --------------------------------------------------------

alignment_results <- purrr::map(
  names(epq_common_item_sets),
  ~ run_alignment_one_scale(.x, all_groups = all_groups, group_key = group_key)
)
names(alignment_results) <- names(epq_common_item_sets)

# ---- 7. EXPORT RESULTS -------------------------------------------------------

aligned_means <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "pars"))
alignment_es  <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "es"))
alignment_r2  <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "r2_tbl"))
r2_summary    <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "r2_summary"))
noninv_tbl    <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "noninv"))
config_lambda <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "config_lambda"))
config_nu     <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "config_nu"))

readr::write_csv(aligned_means, file.path(OUT_DIR, "aligned_factor_means_variances.csv"))
readr::write_csv(alignment_es,  file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_effect_sizes.csv"))
readr::write_csv(alignment_r2,  file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_r2_long.csv"))
readr::write_csv(r2_summary,    file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_r2_summary.csv"))
readr::write_csv(noninv_tbl,    file.path(OUT_DIR, "noninvariant_params.csv"))
readr::write_csv(config_lambda, file.path(OUT_DIR, "configural_loadings.csv"))
readr::write_csv(config_nu,     file.path(OUT_DIR, "configural_nu.csv"))

# ---- 8. BANGLA PERCENTILE ----------------------------------------------------

bangla_percentile <- aligned_means |>
  dplyr::group_by(scale) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    z_against_groups = as.numeric(scale(mean_eng_ref)),
    percentile = dplyr::percent_rank(mean_eng_ref) * 100
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(country == BANGLA_LABEL)

readr::write_csv(bangla_percentile, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bangla_percentile.csv"))

# ---- 9. SUMMARIES ------------------------------------------------------------

noninv_summary <- noninv_tbl |>
  dplyr::group_by(scale, parameter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    flagged_n = sum(flagged, na.rm = TRUE),
    total = dplyr::n(),
    pct_flagged = 100 * flagged_n / total,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

readr::write_csv(noninv_summary, file.path(OUT_DIR, "noninvariance_summary.csv"))

# ---- 10. PLOT ----------------------------------------------------------------

plot_df <- aligned_means |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    is_bangla = country == BANGLA_LABEL,
    scale = factor(scale, levels = c("Psychoticism", "Extraversion", "Neuroticism", "Lie"))
  )

p <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  plot_df,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = mean_eng_ref,
    y = reorder(country, mean_eng_ref),
    colour = is_bangla,
    size = is_bangla
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ scale, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "grey60", "TRUE" = "#C0392B"),
    labels = c("Other group", BANGLA_LABEL),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_size_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 1.8, "TRUE" = 4), guide = "none") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Aligned factor means relative to England",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "R-only approximation to McLarnon/Web Note 18 logic; England reference file = ",
      ENGLAND_FILE
    ),
    x = "Aligned latent mean (England = 0)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold")
  )

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(OUT_DIR, "benchmark_plot.pdf"),
  plot = p,
  width = 12,
  height = max(10, 0.22 * dplyr::n_distinct(plot_df$country))
)

# ---- 11. SAVE WORKSPACE ------------------------------------------------------

saveRDS(
  list(
    group_key = group_key,
    alignment_results = alignment_results,
    aligned_means = aligned_means,
    alignment_es = alignment_es,
    alignment_r2 = alignment_r2,
    r2_summary = r2_summary,
    noninv_tbl = noninv_tbl,
    noninv_summary = noninv_summary,
    bangla_percentile = bangla_percentile
  ),
  file = file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_workspace.rds")
)

print(r2_summary)
```
